title: Best Eva Collections
wikitext:
[]: Best Eva Collections             Quote Post by gcampos » Wed Nov 07, 2012 5:16 pm Hi, I'm a long time big Eva fan (like a a lot of people here), and after read the manga again for the fourth time, I decide that I want to re watch the whole series again. Do you guys know what is the best option to buy the whole Eva original series + the end of evangelion movies? I was thinking about wait for a blu ray release, but I found a thread here about 16mm stuff and saying that a blu ray version wouldn't be much better than a dvd.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Nov 07, 2012 5:57 pm Currently there's no Blu Ray release nor known plans for an immediate release, so if you want to buy it right now the 16mm thing would be a moot point. You didn't specify your country but assuming that you're American you should hurry up to buy the Platinum from Right Stuf or wherever you can find it because I read that it's going to be OOP, also it's really quite cheap right now. EoE is already very hard to find, it's quite expensive and the A/V quality of Manga release sucks greatly. If you want to own it just because you want to support the "industry" or something like that then you should probably buy the Japanese edition from amazon.co.jp, cdjapan or some other store, there's also the option of the Italian edition from amazon.it which is the only western release of the feature films based on the Renewal masters afaik. Keep in mind that both Japanese and Italian DVDs are incompatible with R1 region locked hardware players (most DVD players are region free nowadays but you can't use the PS3 for example).

[]:             Quote Post by gcampos » Wed Nov 07, 2012 6:09 pm Thanks dude :) So the platinum version is only the series right ? I really want the EoE because the end of the serie is... well... you know hehe ;) I have a Samsung bluray, but Im not sure if is dvd region free or not. Does the Japanese version have English subtitles? Btw, I'm not American, I'm Brazilian, but I do live in US, so your assumption was not that bad hehe

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Nov 07, 2012 6:14 pm gcampos wrote: Does the Japanese version have English subtitles?

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Nov 07, 2012 6:17 pm gcampos wrote: Btw, I'm not American, I'm Brazilian, but I do live in US, so your assumption was not that bad hehe

[]:             Quote Post by gcampos » Wed Nov 07, 2012 6:21 pm I could try to look for a Brazilian version, they might have one and I don't know... Anyway thanks again, at least now I know what I should look for :)
